title: On The Same. (On A Henpecked Country Squire.)
content:
O Death, hadst thou but spared his life,
            Whom we this day lament,
        We freely wad exchang'd the wife,
            And a' been weel content!

        Ev'n as he is, cauld in his graff,
            The swap we yet will do't;
        Take thou the carlin's carcase aff,
            Thou'se get the soul to boot.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "On The Same. (On A Henpecked Country Squire.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
